title: Peer Tutoring Request
transcript: Student: Hey, I heard you're really good at calculus. Would you be willing to tutor me before the midterm? Tutor: Sure, I'd be happy to help. When were you thinking? Student: Maybe this weekend? I'm free Saturday afternoon and all day Sunday. Tutor: Saturday afternoon works for me. Let's meet at the library at 2 PM. Student: Great. Should I bring anything specific? Tutor: Bring your textbook, your notes, and any practice problems you've been struggling with. Also, it helps if you identify the topics you find most confusing. Student: Definitely integration by parts and related rates. Tutor: Perfect. We'll start there. I usually charge fifteen dollars an hour, but since we're classmates, ten is fine. Student: That sounds fair. Can we do two hours? Tutor: Sure. If you need more sessions after that, just let me know. Student: Thanks so much. I really need to pass this exam.

questions.stem: Why does the student approach their classmate?
options: To ask for tutoring help before the midterm
questions.explanation: The student asks the classmate to tutor them in calculus before the midterm.

questions.stem: What does the tutor tell the student to bring?
options: Previous exam papers and a laptop
Textbook, notes, and difficult practice problems
questions.explanation: The tutor asks the student to bring the textbook, notes, and any practice problems they've been struggling with.

Great. Should I bring anything specific?
Bring your textbook, your notes, and any practice problems you've been struggling with. Also, it helps if you identify the topics you find most confusing.
Definitely integration by parts and related rates.
Perfect. We'll start there. I usually charge fifteen dollars an hour, but since we're classmates, ten is fine.
That sounds fair. Can we do two hours?
Sure. If you need more sessions after that, just let me know.
Thanks so much. I really need to pass this exam.
